1: import { ORPCError } from "@orpc/server";
2: import { hashPassword } from "better-auth/crypto";

17: 
18: export const createUser = protectedProcedure
19:   .meta({
20:     permission: {
21:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
22:       resource: "team_members" as const,
23:     },
24:   })
25:   .input(createUserSchema)
26:   .handler(async ({ context, input }) => {
27:     if (input.role === "custom") {
28:       if (!input.permissions || Object.keys(input.permissions).length === 0) {
29:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
30:           message: "permissions must be non-empty when role is 'custom'",
31:         });
32:       }
33:     }
34: 
35:     if (input.role !== "admin" && !input.supervisorId) {
36:       throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
37:         message: "Non-admin users must have a supervisor",
38:       });
39:     }
40: 
41:     if (input.role === "admin") {
42:       input.supervisorId = undefined;
43:     }
44: 
45:     const normalizedEmail = input.email.toLowerCase();
46: 
47:     const [existing] = await db
48:       .select({ id: user.id })
49:       .from(user)
50:       .where(eq(user.email, normalizedEmail));
51: 
52:     if (existing) {
53:       throw new ORPCError("CONFLICT", {
54:         message: "A user with this email already exists",
55:       });
56:     }
57: 
58:     const id = crypto.randomUUID();
59: 
60:     if (input.supervisorId) {
61:       const [supervisor] = await db
62:         .select({ id: user.id })
63:         .from(user)
64:         .where(eq(user.id, input.supervisorId));
65: 
66:       if (!supervisor) {
67:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
68:           message: "Supervisor not found",
69:         });
70:       }
71: 
72:       await validateNoCircularSupervisor(
73:         id,
74:         input.supervisorId,
75:         context.subordinateCache,
76:       );
77:     }
78: 
79:     await db.insert(user).values({
80:       email: normalizedEmail,
81:       id,
82:       name: input.name,
83:       permissions: input.role === "custom" ? input.permissions : null,
84:       role: input.role,
85:       supervisorId: input.supervisorId ?? null,
86:     });
87: 
88:     const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(input.password);
89:     await db.insert(account).values({
90:       accountId: id,
91:       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
92:       password: hashedPassword,
93:       providerId: "credential",
94:       userId: id,
95:     });
96: 
97:     return { id };
98:   });
99: 
100: export const updateUser = protectedProcedure
101:   .meta({
102:     permission: {
103:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
104:       resource: "team_members" as const,
105:     },
106:   })
107:   .input(updateUserSchema)
108:   .handler(async ({ context, input }) => {
109:     const {
110:       userId,
111:       role,
112:       permissions,
113:       banned,
114:       banReason,
115:       banExpires,
116:       name,
117:       email,
118:       supervisorId,
119:     } = input;
120: 
121:     if (role === "custom") {
122:       if (!permissions || Object.keys(permissions).length === 0) {
123:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
124:           message: "permissions must be non-empty when role is 'custom'",
125:         });
126:       }
127:     }
128: 
129:     const [existing] = await db
130:       .select({ role: user.role, supervisorId: user.supervisorId })
131:       .from(user)
132:       .where(eq(user.id, userId));
133: 
134:     if (!existing) {
135:       throw new ORPCError("NOT_FOUND", { message: "User not found" });
136:     }
137: 
138:     const effectiveRole = role ?? existing.role;
139:     const effectiveSupervisorId =
140:       supervisorId !== undefined ? supervisorId : existing.supervisorId;
141: 
142:     if (effectiveRole !== "admin" && !effectiveSupervisorId) {
143:       throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
144:         message: "Non-admin users must have a supervisor",
145:       });
146:     }
147: 
148:     if (supervisorId) {
149:       const [supervisor] = await db
150:         .select({ id: user.id })
151:         .from(user)
152:         .where(eq(user.id, supervisorId));
153: 
154:       if (!supervisor) {
155:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
156:           message: "Supervisor not found",
157:         });
158:       }
159: 
160:       await validateNoCircularSupervisor(
161:         userId,
162:         supervisorId,
163:         context.subordinateCache,
164:       );
165:     }
166: 
167:     const updateData: Partial<typeof user.$inferInsert> = {};
168: 
169:     if (role !== undefined) {
170:       updateData.role = role;
171:     }
172: 
173:     if (banned === true) {
174:       updateData.banned = true;
175:       updateData.banReason = banReason ?? null;
176:       updateData.banExpires = banExpires ?? null;
177:     } else if (banned === false) {
178:       updateData.banned = false;
179:       updateData.banReason = null;
180:       updateData.banExpires = null;
181:     }
182: 
183:     if (permissions !== undefined) {
184:       updateData.permissions =
185:         role === "custom" || (role === undefined && permissions)
186:           ? permissions
187:           : null;
188:     }
189:     if (name !== undefined) updateData.name = name;
190:     if (email !== undefined) updateData.email = email;
191: 
192:     if (supervisorId !== undefined) {
193:       updateData.supervisorId = supervisorId;
194:     }
195: 
196:     if (Object.keys(updateData).length > 0) {
197:       await db.update(user).set(updateData).where(eq(user.id, userId));
198:     }
199: 
200:     if (banned === true) {
201:       await db.delete(session).where(eq(session.userId, userId));
202:     }
203: 
204:     if (role !== undefined || permissions !== undefined) {
205:       await db.delete(session).where(eq(session.userId, userId));
206:     }
207: 
208:     return { success: true };
209:   });

62: };
63: 
64: const COLUMN_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
65:   archived: "archived",
66:   assigneeId: "assignee_id",
67:   clientId: "client_id",
68:   description: "description",
69:   endDate: "end_date",
70:   pdfLink: "pdf_link",
71:   referenceNumber: "reference_number",
72:   rmId: "rm_id",
73:   signedDate: "signed_date",
74:   startDate: "start_date",
75:   status: "status",
76:   title: "title",
77: };
78: 
79: const UPDATABLE_FIELDS = [
80:   "title",
81:   "description",
82:   "clientId",
83:   "assigneeId",
84:   "rmId",
85:   "startDate",
86:   "endDate",
87:   "signedDate",
88:   "pdfLink",
89:   "referenceNumber",
90:   "status",
91:   "archived",
92: ] as const;
93: 
94: const bdUser = alias(user, "bd_user");
95: const rmUser = alias(user, "rm_user");
96: 
97: async function recordContractEvent(
98:   contractId: number,
99:   field: string,
100:   oldValue: string | null,
101:   newValue: string | null,
102:   changedBy: string,
103: ) {
104:   await db.insert(contractEvents).values({
105:     changedBy,
106:     contractId,
107:     field,
108:     newValue,
109:     oldValue,
110:   });
111: }
112: 
113: function buildContractSelect() {
114:   return {
115:     archived: clientContracts.archived,
116:     assigneeId: clientContracts.assigneeId,
117:     bdImage: bdUser.image,
118:     bdName: bdUser.name,
119:     clientId: clientContracts.clientId,
120:     clientName: clients.name,
121:     createdAt: clientContracts.createdAt,
122:     description: clientContracts.description,
123:     endDate: clientContracts.endDate,
124:     id: clientContracts.id,
125:     pdfLink: clientContracts.pdfLink,
126:     referenceNumber: clientContracts.referenceNumber,
127:     rmId: clientContracts.rmId,
128:     rmImage: rmUser.image,
129:     rmName: rmUser.name,
130:     signedDate: clientContracts.signedDate,
131:     startDate: clientContracts.startDate,
132:     status: clientContracts.status,
133:     title: clientContracts.title,
134:     updatedAt: clientContracts.updatedAt,
135:   };
136: }
137: 
138: export async function createContract(
139:   input: CreateContractInput,
140:   userId: string,
141: ) {
142:   const [contract] = await db
143:     .insert(clientContracts)
144:     .values({
145:       assigneeId: input.assigneeId,
146:       clientId: input.clientId,
147:       description: input.description ?? null,
148:       endDate: input.endDate ?? null,
149:       pdfLink: input.pdfLink ?? null,
150:       referenceNumber: input.referenceNumber ?? null,
151:       rmId: input.rmId ?? null,
152:       signedDate: input.signedDate ?? null,
153:       startDate: input.startDate ?? null,
154:       status: input.status ?? "active",
155:       title: input.title,
156:     })
157:     .returning();
158: 
159:   const fieldsToRecord: [string, string | null][] = [
160:     ["title", input.title],
161:     ["description", input.description ?? null],
162:     ["client_id", input.clientId != null ? String(input.clientId) : null],
163:     ["assignee_id", input.assigneeId],
164:     ["rm_id", input.rmId ?? null],
165:     ["start_date", input.startDate ?? null],
166:     ["end_date", input.endDate ?? null],
167:     ["signed_date", input.signedDate ?? null],
168:     ["pdf_link", input.pdfLink ?? null],
169:     ["reference_number", input.referenceNumber ?? null],
170:     ["status", input.status ?? "active"],
171:   ];
172: 
173:   for (const [field, value] of fieldsToRecord) {
174:     if (value !== null) {
175:       await recordContractEvent(contract.id, field, null, value, userId);
176:     }
177:   }
178: 
179:   return getContract(contract.id);
180: }
181: 
182: export async function updateContract(
183:   id: number,
184:   input: UpdateContractInput,
185:   userId: string,
186: ) {
187:   const [existing] = await db
188:     .select()
189:     .from(clientContracts)
190:     .where(eq(clientContracts.id, id));
191: 
192:   if (!existing) throw new Error("Contract not found");
193: 
194:   const updateData: Record<string, unknown> = {};
195: 
196:   for (const field of UPDATABLE_FIELDS) {
197:     if (input[field] !== undefined) {
198:       const oldVal = existing[field as keyof typeof existing];
199:       const newVal = input[field];
200: 
201:       const oldStr = oldVal === null ? null : String(oldVal);
202:       const newStr = newVal === null ? null : String(newVal);
203: 
204:       if (oldStr !== newStr) {
205:         updateData[field] = newVal;
206:         await recordContractEvent(
207:           id,
208:           COLUMN_MAP[field],
209:           oldStr,
210:           newStr,
211:           userId,
212:         );
213:       }
214:     }
215:   }
216: 
217:   if (Object.keys(updateData).length > 0) {
218:     updateData.updatedAt = new Date();
219:     await db
220:       .update(clientContracts)
221:       .set(updateData)
222:       .where(eq(clientContracts.id, id));
223:   }
224: 
225:   return getContract(id);
226: }
227: 
228: export async function archiveContract(id: number, userId: string) {
229:   return updateContract(id, { archived: true }, userId);
230: }
231: 
232: export async function getContract(id: number) {
233:   const [row] = await db
234:     .select(buildContractSelect())
235:     .from(clientContracts)
236:     .leftJoin(bdUser, eq(clientContracts.assigneeId, bdUser.id))
237:     .leftJoin(rmUser, eq(clientContracts.rmId, rmUser.id))
238:     .leftJoin(clients, eq(clientContracts.clientId, clients.id))
239:     .where(eq(clientContracts.id, id));
240: 
241:   if (!row) return null;
242: 
243:   return {
244:     ...row,
245:     bd: {
246:       id: row.assigneeId,
247:       image: row.bdImage,
248:       name: row.bdName,
249:     },
250:     client: {
251:       id: row.clientId,
252:       name: row.clientName,
253:     },
254:     rm: row.rmId
255:       ? { id: row.rmId, image: row.rmImage, name: row.rmName }
256:       : null,
257:   };
258: }
259: 
260: export async function listContracts(input: ListContractsInput = {}) {
261:   const {
262:     filters = {},
263:     limit = 20,
264:     sortBy = "createdAt",
265:     sortOrder = "desc",
266:   } = input;
267: 
268:   const conditions = [];
269: 
270:   if (filters.archived !== undefined) {
271:     conditions.push(eq(clientContracts.archived, filters.archived));
272:   } else {
273:     conditions.push(eq(clientContracts.archived, false));
274:   }
275: 
276:   if (filters.clientId !== undefined) {
277:     conditions.push(eq(clientContracts.clientId, filters.clientId));
278:   }
279: 
280:   if (filters.status?.length) {
281:     conditions.push(inArray(clientContracts.status, filters.status));
282:   }
283: 
284:   if (filters.assigneeId?.length) {
285:     conditions.push(inArray(clientContracts.assigneeId, filters.assigneeId));
286:   }
287: 
288:   if (filters.search) {
289:     const pattern = `%${filters.search}%`;
290:     conditions.push(
291:       sql`(${ilike(clientContracts.title, pattern)} OR ${ilike(clientContracts.referenceNumber, pattern)})`,
292:     );
293:   }
294: 
295:   const cursorId = input.cursor ? Number.parseInt(input.cursor, 10) : null;
296:   if (cursorId !== null && !Number.isNaN(cursorId)) {
297:     if (sortOrder === "asc") {
298:       conditions.push(sql`${clientContracts.id} > ${cursorId}`);
299:     } else {
300:       conditions.push(sql`${clientContracts.id} < ${cursorId}`);
301:     }
302:   }
303: 
304:   const whereClause = conditions.length > 0 ? and(...conditions) : undefined;
305: 
306:   const sortColumn =
307:     sortBy === "title"
308:       ? clientContracts.title
309:       : sortBy === "startDate"
310:         ? clientContracts.startDate
311:         : sortBy === "endDate"
312:           ? clientContracts.endDate
313:           : clientContracts.createdAt;
314: 
315:   const orderFn = sortOrder === "asc" ? asc(sortColumn) : desc(sortColumn);
316: 
317:   const rows = await db
318:     .select(buildContractSelect())
319:     .from(clientContracts)
320:     .leftJoin(bdUser, eq(clientContracts.assigneeId, bdUser.id))
321:     .leftJoin(rmUser, eq(clientContracts.rmId, rmUser.id))
322:     .leftJoin(clients, eq(clientContracts.clientId, clients.id))
323:     .where(whereClause)
324:     .orderBy(orderFn)
325:     .limit(limit + 1);
326: 
327:   const hasMore = rows.length > limit;
328:   const items = hasMore ? rows.slice(0, limit) : rows;
329: 
330:   const enriched = items.map((row) => ({
331:     ...row,
332:     bd: {
333:       id: row.assigneeId,
334:       image: row.bdImage,
335:       name: row.bdName,
336:     },
337:     client: {
338:       id: row.clientId,
339:       name: row.clientName,
340:     },
341:     rm: row.rmId
342:       ? { id: row.rmId, image: row.rmImage, name: row.rmName }
343:       : null,
344:   }));
345: 
346:   const nextCursor =
347:     hasMore && enriched.length > 0
348:       ? String(enriched[enriched.length - 1].id)
349:       : null;
350: 
351:   return { items: enriched, nextCursor };
352: }
353: 
354: export async function listContractEvents(
355:   contractId: number,
356:   _cursor?: string,
357:   limit = 50,
358: ) {
359:   const rows = await db
360:     .select()
361:     .from(contractEvents)
362:     .where(eq(contractEvents.contractId, contractId))
363:     .orderBy(desc(contractEvents.changedAt))
364:     .limit(limit + 1);
365: 
366:   const hasMore = rows.length > limit;
367:   const items = hasMore ? rows.slice(0, limit) : rows;
368: 
369:   return {
370:     items,
371:     nextCursor:
372:       hasMore && items.length > 0 ? String(items[items.length - 1].id) : null,
373:   };
374: }

377:   .meta({ permission: { action: "create", resource: "client_contracts" } })
378:   .input(ClientContract_FormSchema)
379:   .handler(async ({ context, input }) => {

383:       context.user.id,

387:

parts.text:
The typecheck errors related to my changes are:

1. **`src/rpc/router/admin.ts`** - `@/schema` and `@/schema/auth-form` module resolution errors, and `createUserSchema` not found. The re-export `export { memberFormSchema as createUserSchema } from "@/schema/auth-form"` isn't resolving. Let me check what `@/schema` resolves to.

2. **`src/rpc/router/client-contract.ts`** - `ClientContract_FormSchema` not found. The re-export `export { ClientContract_FormSchema } from "@/lib/form-schemas/client-contract"` should be resolving, but the error says "Cannot find name 'ClientContract_FormSchema'". Wait, the error is at line 419 which is `.input(ClientContract_FormSchema)` - it seems like the import isn't being picked up. Let me look more carefully at the error.

3. **`src/rpc/router/client.ts`** - `@/schema/client-form` module resolution error, `createClientSchema` and `updateClientSchema` not found.

4. **`src/rpc/router/users.ts`** - `@/schema` module resolution error.

Wait, I see the pattern now. Many errors say "Cannot find module '@/schema'" ã these are pre-existing errors unrelated to my changes. The errors specific to my changes are the ones about `createUserSchema`, `ClientContract_FormSchema`, `createClientSchema`, and `updateClientSchema` not being found in the router files.

Let me look at the admin.ts error more closely:
- `src/rpc/router/admin.ts(10,40): error TS2307: Cannot find module '@/schema' or its corresponding type declarations.` - This is line 10 which has `import { account, session, user } from "@/schema";`
- `src/rpc/router/admin.ts(13,54): error TS2307: Cannot find module '@/schema/auth-form' or its corresponding type declarations.` - This is the re-export line
- `src/rpc/router/admin.ts(49,10): error TS2552: Cannot find name 'createUserSchema'. Did you mean 'updateUserSchema'?` - The `createUserSchema` is used but not found

Wait, the `@/schema` import was there in the original admin.ts too. Let me re-read the original admin.ts...

Looking at the original admin.ts:
```
import { account, session, user } from "@/schema";
```

And the schema/admin.ts had:
```
export { memberFormSchema as createUserSchema } from "@/schema/auth-form";
```

So the original admin.ts imported `createUserSchema` from `@/rpc/schema/admin`, and the schema file re-exported it from `@/schema/auth-form`.

In my new admin.ts, I have:
```
export { memberFormSchema as createUserSchema } from "@/schema/auth-form";
```

And then `createUserSchema` is used in the `.input(createUserSchema)` call. The issue is that the re-export IS in the file, but maybe the tsgo compiler can't find the `@/schema/auth-form` module?

Wait, looking at the errors, it says `Cannot find module '@/schema/auth-form' or its corresponding type declarations.` This means `@/schema/auth-form` doesn't exist as a module. Let me check what the correct path is.

Looking at the original `schema/admin.ts`:
```
export { memberFormSchema as createUserSchema } from "@/schema/auth-form";
```

And the search earlier showed that `memberFormSchema` is actually defined in `@/lib/form-schemas/auth.ts`. So there must be a re-export somewhere that maps `@/schema/auth-form` to the actual file.

Wait, let me check if there's a `src/schema/` directory that has these re-exports.
The typecheck shows some import resolution errors. The re-exports from `@/schema/auth-form`, `@/schema/client-form`, and `@/schema` are failing. Let me check the actual module paths:

OK so the type errors related to `@/schema` and `@/schema/auth-form` and `@/schema/client-form` are **pre-existing** - they existed before my changes. The `src/schema/` directory was removed in a prior refactoring but the imports weren't updated.

The specific errors caused by my changes are:

1. **`src/rpc/router/admin.ts`**: The `export { memberFormSchema as createUserSchema } from "@/schema/auth-form"` re-export doesn't make `createUserSchema` available in the local scope. I need to import it separately AND the module path is wrong (`@/schema/auth-form` doesn't exist).

2. **`src/rpc/router/client-contract.ts`**: `export { ClientContract_FormSchema } from "@/lib/form-schemas/client-contract"` re-export doesn't make it available locally.

3. **`src/rpc/router/client.ts`**: The `export { clientFormSchema as createClientSchema, clientUpdateFormSchema as updateClientSchema } from "@/schema/client-form"` has the wrong module path and re-export scope issue.

I need to fix these by:
1. Using the correct import paths (from `@/lib/form-schemas/...` instead of `@/schema/...`)
2. Using `import` instead of just `export { ... } from` since the values need to be available in the local scope

Let me also check what the correct module paths should be:
- `memberFormSchema` ã `@/lib/form-schemas/auth`
- `clientFormSchema`, `clientUpdateFormSchema` ã `@/lib/form-schemas/client`
- `ClientContract_FormSchema` ã `@/lib/form-schemas/client-contract`

For admin.ts:
```typescript
import { memberFormSchema as createUserSchema } from "@/lib/form-schemas/auth";
export { createUserSchema };
```

For client-contract.ts:
```typescript
import { ClientContract_FormSchema } from "@/lib/form-schemas/client-contract";
export { ClientContract_FormSchema };
```

For client.ts:
```typescript
import { clientFormSchema as createClientSchema, clientUpdateFormSchema as updateClientSchema } from "@/lib/form-schemas/client";
export { createClientSchema, updateClientSchema };
```

Wait, but does anyone else import these re-exports from the router files? Let me check... The grep earlier only showed the router files importing from `@/rpc/schema/`, and no one else. But now that these schemas are in the router files, they might be imported from there. Let me check if anyone imports `createUserSchema`, `createClientSchema`, etc. from the router...

Actually, the router index does `import * as admin from "./admin"` which means all exports from admin.ts are accessible as `admin.updateUserSchema`, `admin.createUserSchema`, etc. But the oRPC router structure uses these as procedures, not as schema exports. The issue is that the `import * as admin` brings in ALL exports including the schemas, which then pollute the router type and cause the "Property 'updateUserSchema' is incompatible with index signature" error.

This is actually a critical issue! The `router/index.ts` does:
```typescript
import * as admin from "./admin";
```

And then:
```typescript
export const router = {
  admin,
  ...
};
```

The oRPC router expects each namespace to contain only procedures (or nested namespaces). But now that the schemas are in the same file, `import * as admin` also includes the schema exports, which breaks the router type.

So the real fix is to NOT export the schemas from the router files, or to change the router index to only import the procedures. Let me think about the best approach:

**Option 1**: Don't export schemas at all - just define them as `const` without `export`. But they might be needed elsewhere (e.g., `createUserSchema` is used by `admin.ts` router itself, so it just needs to be in scope).

**Option 2**: Export only what's needed, and change `router/index.ts` to not use `import * as`.

Actually, looking at `router/index.ts`, the `admin` namespace is imported as `import * as admin from "./admin"` and used directly in the router. The other namespaces (client, contract, etc.) are manually constructed by picking specific procedures. Only `admin` uses the wildcard import.

The problem: `import * as admin from "./admin"` picks up ALL exports including the schema constants. When this is passed to the oRPC router, the type checker complains because schemas don't match the expected procedure type.

So I have two choices:
1. Remove `export` from schemas that are only used within their own file (keep them as non-exported `const`)
2. Change the admin router index import to explicitly pick procedures like the other namespaces

Looking at the original code:
- `admin.ts` used `import * as admin from "./admin"` even before my changes
- The original `admin.ts` didn't export schemas because they were in the separate `schema/admin.ts` file
- Now that I've moved schemas into `admin.ts`, the `import * as admin` picks them up

The cleanest fix: remove `export` from schemas that don't need to be imported by other files. Let me check which schemas are used across files:

1. `reminder.ts` imports `archiveReminderSchema`, `createReminderSchema`, `listRemindersSchema` from `./task` - these NEED to be exported
2. No other cross-file schema imports exist

So the fix is:
- **admin.ts**: Remove `export` from `updateUserSchema`, `archiveUserSchema`, `resetPasswordSchema`. For `createUserSchema`, just import it locally (no export needed).
- **client-contract.ts**: Remove `export` from schemas. Import `ClientContract_FormSchema` locally (no export needed).
- **client.ts**: Remove `export` from schemas. Import `createClientSchema` and `updateClientSchema` locally (no export needed).
- **notification.ts**: Remove `export` from schemas.
- **prospect.ts**: Remove `export` from schemas.
- **users.ts**: Remove `export` from `listUsersSchema`.
- **task.ts**: Keep `export` on `createReminderSchema`, `archiveReminderSchema`, `listRemindersSchema` (used by reminder.ts). Remove `export` from others that are only used within task.ts itself.

Wait, let me re-check. The `contractStatusSchema` in client-contract.ts is used in `listContractsSchema` and `updateContractSchema`, and it's also exported. Let me check if anything imports it...

Looking at the grep output earlier, `contractStatusSchema` is only used within `schema/client-contract.ts` itself. So I can remove the export.

Similarly, `taskStatusSchema` is used within `task.ts` and in `listTasksSchema`. But it might be needed elsewhere... let me check.

Actually, looking at the grep for `taskStatusSchema`, it was only used in the schema file itself. So I can remove the export.

Let me also check if `listUsersSchema` from `users.ts` is imported anywhere else besides the router file itself.

Looking at the grep, `listUsersSchema` was only imported by `router/users.ts`.

OK so the plan is:
1. Remove `export` from all schemas that are only used within their own router file
2. Keep `export` only for schemas used by other files (reminder.ts imports from task.ts)
3. Fix the import paths for re-exported form schemas (use `@/lib/form-schemas/...` instead of `@/schema/...`)
4. For form schemas that are used locally (as `.input()`), use `import` not `export { ... } from`

Let me also address the Biome lint issue about import order. The `import { z } from "zod"` should come before `@/` imports (organized by Biome).

Let me rewrite all affected files now.

The `@/schema/*` errors are pre-existing, but the new errors are:
1. Re-exports (`export { X } from '...'`) don't make `X` available in local scope ã need `import` instead
2. `import * as admin` in `router/index.ts` picks up schema exports, polluting the router type

I need to: use `import` (not re-export) for locally-used schemas, remove `export` from schemas that don't need cross-file visibility, and keep exports only for schemas used by `reminder.ts`.
